table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Informações sobre a utilização do elemento

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Número mínimo e máximo de vezes que o elemento pode aparecer na instância

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px; width: 100px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

tr.td: Data e horário de início e término da visita

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Motivo da visita ou do acompanhamento

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck00.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

description.value: Visita ou atendimento em campo no qual o Agente Comunitário de Saúde aplica ou atualiza a anamnese AgroSUS.

short.value: An interaction during which services are provided to the patient

definition.value: An interaction between a patient and healthcare provider(s) for the purpose of providing healthcare service(s) or assessing the health status of a patient.

human.value: If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT contain nested Resources
If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL be referred to from elsewhere in the resource or SHALL refer to the containing resource

expression.value: contained.where((('#'+id in (%resource.descendants().reference | %resource.descendants().as(canonical) | %resource.descendants().as(uri) | %resource.descendants().as(url))) or descendants().where(reference = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists()).not()).trace('unmatched', id).empty()

xpath.value: not(exists(for $id in f:contained/*/f:id/@value return $contained[not(parent::*/descendant::f:reference/@value=concat('#', $contained/*/id/@value) or descendant::f:reference[@value='#'])]))

human.value: If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a meta.versionId or a meta.lastUpdated
If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a security label

valueMarkdown.value: When a resource has no narrative, only systems that fully understand the data can display the resource to a human safely. Including a human readable representation in the resource makes for a much more robust eco-system and cheaper handling of resources by intermediary systems. Some ecosystems restrict distribution of resources to only those systems that do fully understand the resources, and as a consequence implementers may believe that the narrative is superfluous. However experience shows that such eco-systems often open up to new participants over time.

human.value: A resource should have narrative for robust management

short.value: Identificador lógico deste artefato

definition.value: Identificador lógico deste artefato

comment.value: The only time that a resource does not have an id is when it is being submitted to the server using a create operation.

short.value: Metadados sobre o recurso

definition.value: Metadados sobre o recurso

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Um conjunto de regras sob as quais este conteúdo foi criado

definition.value: Um conjunto de regras sob as quais este conteúdo foi criado

comment.value: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of it's narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies it's meaning or interpretation

short.value: Idioma do recurso

definition.value: Idioma do recurso

comment.value: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Resumo do texto do recurso em linguagem natural

definition.value: Resumo do texto do recurso em linguagem natural

comment.value: Contained resources do not have narrative. Resources that are not contained SHOULD have a narrative. In some cases, a resource may only have text with little or no additional discrete data (as long as all minOccurs=1 elements are satisfied). This may be necessary for data from legacy systems where information is captured as a "text blob" or where text is additionally entered raw or narrated and encoded information is added later.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Recursos contidos no recurso

definition.value: Recursos contidos no recurso

comment.value: This should never be done when the content can be identified properly, as once identification is lost, it is extremely difficult (and context dependent) to restore it again. Contained resources may have profiles and tags In their meta elements, but SHALL NOT have security labels.

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo base

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo base

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the resource that contains them

short.value: Identificador único da visita AgroSUS

definition.value: Identificadores externos para este recurso

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: usual | official | temp | secondary | old (If known)

definition.value: The purpose of this identifier.

comment.value: Applications can assume that an identifier is permanent unless it explicitly says that it is temporary.

requirements.value: Allows the appropriate identifier for a particular context of use to be selected from among a set of identifiers.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not mistake a temporary id for a permanent one.

description.value: Identifies the purpose for this identifier, if known .

short.value: Description of identifier

definition.value: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

comment.value: This element deals only with general categories of identifiers. It SHOULD not be used for codes that correspond 1..1 with the Identifier.system. Some identifiers may fall into multiple categories due to common usage. Where the system is known, a type is unnecessary because the type is always part of the system definition. However systems often need to handle identifiers where the system is not known. There is not a 1:1 relationship between type and system, since many different systems have the same type.

requirements.value: Allows users to make use of identifiers when the identifier system is not known.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: A coded type for an identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

short.value: The namespace for the identifier value

definition.value: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, a URL that describes a set values that are unique.

comment.value: Identifier.system is always case sensitive.

requirements.value: There are many sets of identifiers. To perform matching of two identifiers, we need to know what set we're dealing with. The system identifies a particular set of unique identifiers.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The value that is unique

definition.value: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

comment.value: If the value is a full URI, then the system SHALL be urn:ietf:rfc:3986. The value's primary purpose is computational mapping. As a result, it may be normalized for comparison purposes (e.g. removing non-significant whitespace, dashes, etc.) A value formatted for human display can be conveyed using the [Rendered Value extension](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extension-rendered-value.html). Identifier.value is to be treated as case sensitive unless knowledge of the Identifier.system allows the processer to be confident that non-case-sensitive processing is safe.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: II.extension or II.root if system indicates OID or GUID (Or Role.id.extension or root)

short.value: Time period when id is/was valid for use

definition.value: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Organization that issued id (may be just text)

definition.value: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

comment.value: The Identifier.assigner may omit the .reference element and only contain a .display element reflecting the name or other textual information about the assigning organization.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: II.assigningAuthorityName but note that this is an improper use by the definition of the field. Also Role.scoper

short.value: Situação atual da visita

definition.value: Status atual do atendimento: planned | arrived | triaged | in-progress | onleave | finished | cancelled +

comment.value: Note that internal business rules will determine the appropriate transitions that may occur between statuses (and also classes).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This element is labeled as a modifier because it is a status element that contains status entered-in-error which means that the resource should not be treated as valid

map.value: No clear equivalent in HL7 v2; active/finished could be inferred from PV1-44, PV1-45, PV2-24; inactive could be inferred from PV2-16

short.value: Lista de status de atendimentos anteriores

definition.value: Lista de status de atendimentos anteriores

comment.value: The current status is always found in the current version of the resource, not the status history.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

definition.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Status de atendimentos anteriores: planned | arrived | triaged | in-progress | onleave | finished | cancelled +

definition.value: Status de atendimentos anteriores: planned | arrived | triaged | in-progress | onleave | finished | cancelled +

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: O tempo em que o episódio estava no status especificado

definition.value: O tempo em que o episódio estava no status especificado

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Modalidade em que o atendimento ocorreu

definition.value: Classificação do atendimento com o paciente

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Lista de classes de atendimentos anteriores

definition.value: Lista de classes de atendimentos anteriores

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

definition.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Classe dos atendimento anteriore

definition.value: Classe dos atendimento anteriore

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Período do atendimento anteriore

definition.value: Período do atendimento anteriore

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Tipo específico de atendimento realizado

definition.value: Tipo específico do atendimento

comment.value: Since there are many ways to further classify encounters, this element is 0..*.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Tipo específico do serviço

definition.value: Tipo específico do serviço

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Caráter ou prioridade do atendimento

definition.value: Prioridade do atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Trabalhador rural atendido

definition.value: Referência ao paciente ou grupo deste atendimento

comment.value: While the encounter is always about the patient, the patient might not actually be known in all contexts of use, and there may be a group of patients that could be anonymous (such as in a group therapy for Alcoholics Anonymous - where the recording of the encounter could be used for billing on the number of people/staff and not important to the context of the specific patients) or alternately in veterinary care a herd of sheep receiving treatment (where the animals are not individually tracked).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Referência ao episódio de cuidado relacionado a este atendimento

definition.value: Referência ao episódio de cuidado relacionado a este atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Referência ao ServiceRequest que iniciou este atendimento

definition.value: Referência ao ServiceRequest que iniciou este atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: ACS responsável pela visita

definition.value: Participantes no atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

definition.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Função do participante no atendimento

definition.value: Função do participante no atendimento

comment.value: The participant type indicates how an individual participates in an encounter. It includes non-practitioner participants, and for practitioners this is to describe the action type in the context of this encounter (e.g. Admitting Dr, Attending Dr, Translator, Consulting Dr). This is different to the practitioner roles which are functional roles, derived from terms of employment, education, licensing, etc.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Período de atuação do participante

definition.value: Período de atuação do participante

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Vínculo profissional do ACS responsável

definition.value: Referência à profissionais envolvidos no atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Referência ao agendamento desde atendimento

definition.value: Referência ao agendamento desde atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Data e horário de início e término da visita

definition.value: Hora de início e término do atendimento

comment.value: If not (yet) known, the end of the Period may be omitted.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Duração do atendimento

definition.value: Duração do atendimento

comment.value: May differ from the time the Encounter.period lasted because of leave of absence.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: (PV1-45 less PV1-44) iff ( (PV1-44 not empty) and (PV1-45 not empty) ); units in minutes

short.value: Motivo da visita ou do acompanhamento

definition.value: Queixa principal do atendimento

comment.value: For systems that need to know which was the primary diagnosis, these will be marked with the standard extension primaryDiagnosis (which is a sequence value rather than a flag, 1 = primary diagnosis).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: EVN-4 / PV2-3 (note: PV2-3 is nominally constrained to inpatient admissions; HL7 v2 makes no vocabulary suggestions for PV2-3; would not expect PV2 segment or PV2-3 to be in use in all implementations )

short.value: Referência ao motivo do atendimento

definition.value: Referência ao motivo do atendimento

comment.value: For systems that need to know which was the primary diagnosis, these will be marked with the standard extension primaryDiagnosis (which is a sequence value rather than a flag, 1 = primary diagnosis).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: EVN-4 / PV2-3 (note: PV2-3 is nominally constrained to inpatient admissions; HL7 v2 makes no vocabulary suggestions for PV2-3; would not expect PV2 segment or PV2-3 to be in use in all implementations )

short.value: Lista de diagnósticos deste atendimento

definition.value: Lista de diagnósticos deste atendimento

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

definition.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: O diagnóstico ou procedimento relevante para este atendimento

definition.value: O diagnóstico ou procedimento relevante para este atendimento

comment.value: For systems that need to know which was the primary diagnosis, these will be marked with the standard extension primaryDiagnosis (which is a sequence value rather than a flag, 1 = primary diagnosis).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: Resources that would commonly referenced at Encounter.indication would be Condition and/or Procedure. These most closely align with DG1/PRB and PR1 respectively.

short.value: Papel deste diagnóstico no atendimento (por exemplo, internação, faturamento, alta…)

definition.value: Papel deste diagnóstico no atendimento (por exemplo, internação, faturamento, alta…)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Classificação do diagnóstico

definition.value: Classificação do diagnóstico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Referências as contas desde atendimento

definition.value: Referências as contas desde atendimento

comment.value: The billing system may choose to allocate billable items associated with the Encounter to different referenced Accounts based on internal business rules.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Detalhes sobre a internação em um serviço de saúde

definition.value: Detalhes sobre a internação em um serviço de saúde

comment.value: An Encounter may cover more than just the inpatient stay. Contexts such as outpatients, community clinics, and aged care facilities are also included. The duration recorded in the period of this encounter covers the entire scope of this hospitalization record.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

definition.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Identificador de pré-internação

definition.value: Identificador de pré-internação

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Referência ao local/organização de onde o paciente veio antes da internação

definition.value: Referência ao local/organização de onde o paciente veio antes da internação

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: De onde o paciente foi admitido (encaminhamento médico, transferência)

definition.value: De onde o paciente foi admitido (encaminhamento médico, transferência)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: O tipo de readmissão hospitalar (se houver). Se o valor estiver ausente, a internação não será dentificada como uma reinternação

definition.value: O tipo de readmissão hospitalar (se houver). Se o valor estiver ausente, a internação não será dentificada como uma reinternação

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: The reason for re-admission of this hospitalization encounter.

short.value: Preferências alimentares do paciente

definition.value: Preferências alimentares do paciente

comment.value: For example, a patient may request both a dairy-free and nut-free diet preference (not mutually exclusive).

requirements.value: Used to track patient's diet restrictions and/or preference. For a complete description of the nutrition needs of a patient during their stay, one should use the nutritionOrder resource which links to Encounter.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: Medical, cultural or ethical food preferences to help with catering requirements.

short.value: Cortesias especiais (VIP, membro do conselho)

definition.value: Cortesias especiais (VIP, membro do conselho)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Necessidades especiais: cadeira de rodas, tradutor, maca, etc

definition.value: Necessidades especiais: cadeira de rodas, tradutor, maca, etc

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Referência ao local/organização de encaminhamento pós-alta

definition.value: Referência ao local/organização de encaminhamento pós-alta

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Categoria ou tipo de local após a alta

definition.value: Categoria ou tipo de local após a alta

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Lista de locais onde o paciente esteve

definition.value: Lista de locais onde o paciente esteve

comment.value: Virtual encounters can be recorded in the Encounter by specifying a location reference to a location of type "kind" such as "client's home" and an encounter.class = "virtual".

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

definition.value: Identificador único para referência cruzada

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo de dados básico

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

definition.value: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Local onde o atendimento acontece

definition.value: Local onde o atendimento acontece

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: O status do local: planned | active | reserved | completed

definition.value: O status do local: planned | active | reserved | completed

comment.value: When the patient is no longer active at a location, then the period end date is entered, and the status may be changed to completed.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: O tipo físico do local (geralmente o nível na hierarquia do local - quarto, enfermaria, etc.)

definition.value: O tipo físico do local (geralmente o nível na hierarquia do local - quarto, enfermaria, etc.)

comment.value:
This information is de-normalized from the Location resource to support the easier understanding of the encounter resource and processing in messaging or query.
There may be many levels in the hierachy, and this may only pic specific levels that are required for a specific usage scenario.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Período de tempo durante o qual o paciente esteve presente no local

definition.value: Período de tempo durante o qual o paciente esteve presente no local

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: UBS responsável pela visita

definition.value: A organização (instalação) responsável por este encontro

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Outro atendimento do qual este atendimento faz parte

definition.value: Outro atendimento do qual este atendimento faz parte

comment.value: This is also used for associating a child's encounter back to the mother's encounter. Refer to the Notes section in the Patient resource for further details.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identificador único da visita AgroSUS
Situação atual da visita
Modalidade em que o atendimento ocorreu
Tipo específico de atendimento realizado
Caráter ou prioridade do atendimento
Trabalhador rural atendido
ACS responsável pela visita
Vínculo profissional do ACS responsável
Data e horário de início e término da visita
Motivo da visita ou do acompanhamento
UBS responsável pela visita
